data: Inscriptiones Bruttiorum Lucaniae Campaniae Siciliae Sardiniae Latinae. Pars posterior. Inscriptiones Siciliae et Sardiniae

T. Mommsen, Inscriptiones Bruttiorum Lucaniae Campaniae Siciliae Sardiniae Latinae. Pars posterior. Inscriptiones Siciliae et Sardiniae, Corpus Inscriptionum Latinarum, consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae editum, 10.2 (Berlin: G. Reimer, 1883)
Not described by Mommsen, but presumably a plaque, and most likely marble.
The only record of this text appears to be Mommsen's autopsy of it in the Collection Coco in Catania; it has not apparently been seen since.
La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano
Giacomo Manganaro, «La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano», Aufstieg und Niedergang der romischen Welt 2.11.1 (1988): 3–89
Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione
Kalle Korhonen, Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione (Helsinki: Societas Scientiarum Fennica, 2004)
Manganaro, 1988, La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano
Korhonen, 2004, Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of Mommsen in CIL

English translation

Estella Kessler

Sacred to the shades of the underworld. Lucius Arrius Secundus lived for 17 years. The marbleworkers living with him made this.

translation

Estella Kessler

Den Seelen der Unterwelt gewidmet. Lucius Arrius Secundus lebte für 17 Jahre. Die Steinmacher, die mit ihm leben, haben das gemacht.

commentary

As Korhonen notes, convive could also be equivalent to convivae.

Text of Mommsen in CIL

Sacred to the shades of the underworld. Lucius Arrius Secundus lived for 17 years. The marbleworkers living with him made this.

Den Seelen der Unterwelt gewidmet. Lucius Arrius Secundus lebte für 17 Jahre. Die Steinmacher, die mit ihm leben, haben das gemacht.

As Korhonen notes, convive could also be equivalent to convivae.
